data.title: Ulta Beauty Expects Growth to Slow as Consumers Focus on Value — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Ulta Beauty guided for growth to slow this fiscal year as shoppers continue to hunt for value in a competitive market.

Chief Executive Kecia Steelman said consumers were resilient in 2025, but were increasingly discerning with their purchases as they focused on affordability. The cosmetics and fragrances retailer expects those trends to continue this fiscal year, Steelman said, adding that rising global conflicts could also come into play.

"We are optimistic about the opportunities ahead while remaining mindful and cautious as we navigate an environment with ongoing global uncertainty and potential economic volatility," Steelman said during a Thursday call with analysts. "We will control what we can control."

Steelman said the company isn't planning to push more promotions, but recognizes that the beauty industry is competitive given shoppers' growing focus on value.

Ulta expects sales to grow 6% to 7% this fiscal year, compared with a 9.7% increase in the prior fiscal year. The company also forecast earnings per share of $28.05 to $28.55 and comparable sales growth of 2.5% to 3.5%.

Analysts polled by FactSet expect sales of $13.15 billion, or up about 6.1%, and earnings of $28.64 a share.

The stock slid 8%, to $574.25, in after-hours trading. Ahead of the market close, shares had surged 90% over the past year.

The guidance came as Ulta recorded lower profit despite higher revenue in its latest quarter, helped by an increase in average ticket and transactions.

Steelman said Ulta's results were better than planned as the company continued to gain market share.

For the fiscal fourth quarter, Ulta posted a profit of $356.7 million, or $8.01 a share, compared with $393.3 million, or $8.46 a share, a year earlier.

Revenue rose 12% to $3.9 billion, compared with analyst estimates of $3.84 billion.

Same-store sales rose 5.8%, compared with analysts' forecast of 4.3% growth. The growth was driven by an increase in average ticket and transactions, the company said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Ulta Won't Push More Promotions as Shoppers Look for Value, CEO Says — Market Talk
data.description: Ulta Beauty isn't planning to offer more deals this fiscal year, even as consumers remain on the hunt for value, CEO Kecia Steelman says during a call with analysts. "We don't have any plans to accelerate promotion, but we recognize that the environment is competitive. It's dynamic," Steelman says. "There's an increased focus right now out there on value." During 2025, shoppers were focused on affordability and grew increasingly discerning with their purchases, a trend Ulta sees continuing this fiscal year, she says. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)
